channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Louisiana hotel surveillance shows Super Bowl reporter with 'Bourbon Street Hustler' just before death
item.description: The Super Bowl reporter found dead in a Louisiana hotel room on Feb. 5 can be seen in a new security photo released by Kenner police walking with suspect Danette Colbert.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times because of an ongoing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Capitol Police: Ex-aide to Trump's labor secretary nominee 'bypassed' security at congressional building
item.description: A former aide to former U.S. Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer “bypassed" a security screening yesterday at a congressional office building, U.S. Capitol Police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration appeals Maryland judge's ruling blocking birthright citizenship order
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Tuesday said it's appealing a Maryland federal judge's ruling blocking the president's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for people whose parents are not legally in the country.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Virginia residents outsmarted home invaders, stole their getaway car: prosecutor
item.description: Dogs really are a man's best friend – In Norfolk, Virginia one even provided a distraction that allowed a home invasion victim to escape.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins. Trump on Monday announced a 25% tariff on steel and aluminum and promised more import duties to come.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Papa John's debuts bath bomb inspired by its signature garlic sauce
item.description: Papa John’s is giving its fans a new way to enjoy its signature garlic sauce this Valentine’s Day. On Tuesday, the pizza chain announced that they created a limited-edition bath bomb inspired by the famous sauce that comes with every pizza order.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he'll abide by court orders that block parts of his agenda
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he would abide by court rulings if they blocked parts of his agenda amid uproar over comments from some allies about defying the legal system.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: New York mayor vows to regain public's trust after Justice Department orders halt to prosecution
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams vowed to regain the public’s trust Tuesday as the Justice Department moved to halt his criminal corruption case, an extraordinary directive that officials said would free him up to assist in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hollowed-out homes. Cars entombed by mud. Unpeopled roads. Belongings reduced to dirt and debris. It all took a toll on Taylor Schenker. After Hurricane Helene last September, Schenker was upset by the deluge of images of Asheville, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: ANNA FURMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Venezuela sends 2 planes to US to return migrants, signaling a potential improvement in relations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Two Venezuelan planes flew to the United States on Monday and returned home with deported Venezuelans, signaling a possible improvement in relations between longtime diplomatic adversaries and a victory for President Donald Trump in his efforts to get more countries to take the
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Labor unions sue to block DOGE access to sensitive information at US agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of labor unions filed a lawsuit Monday asking a federal court to stop Elon Musk’s team from accessing private data at the Education Department, the Treasury Department and the Office of Personnel Management.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Confusion after emergency alert sent to phones in San Francisco
item.description: (KRON) -- Some cell phone users in San Francisco received an emergency alert Monday afternoon. Phones sounded the emergency alert tone after a notification said that an endangered adult was missing.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration moves to shut down Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau is likely the next federal agency on the chopping block as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk continue the DOGE agenda to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brother of Parkland shooter arrested for trespassing at 3 schools
item.description: Zachary Cruz, the 24-year-old brother of the Parkland school shooter, was arrested Feb. 4 for trespassing at multiple school properties, including Wilson Middle School, Wilson High School, and the Valley Career and Technical Center.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Sutherland, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the possibility of Trump serving a third term
item.description: Murmurs from President Trump and his allies have stirred up questions of the incumbent possibly seeking a third term in office, even as he’s currently constitutionally barred from doing so.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he has directed US Treasury to stop minting new pennies, citing rising cost
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin. “For far too long the United States has minted pennies which literally cost us more than 2 cents.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: So long, penny! Trump orders US to ditch 1-cent coin after decades of complaints
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — The lowly penny, the forgotten mainstay of coin jars and car cupholders everywhere, may soon be no more. President Donald Trump announced Sunday he’s ordered his administration to cease production of the 1-cent coin, whose buying power is long past its prime.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge directs Trump administration to comply with order to unfreeze federal grants
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island ordered the Trump administration to comply with his order to unfreeze federal grants, after attorneys general for several Democratic states claimed the directive was not being fulfilled. In a short order Monday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Former Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel nominated as Ohio's lieutenant governor
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine on Monday nominated former Ohio State Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel to be the state's next lieutenant governor, praising him as a seasoned educator and leader with the skills to take the state's helm if need be.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump has unleashed chaos by distraction upon the international community. That's no accident
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Saudis are furious. The Danes are scrambling. Colombia has backed down. Mexico and Canada stand in a purgatory between tariff wars with the US and … not. China has retaliated, launching a trade war between the economic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets the NFL's season rushing record, including the playoffs
item.description: Saquon Barkley set an NFL single-season rushing record in the Super Bowl, held his young daughter as a torrent of confetti fluttered around them and flashed a smile as he held a Philadelphia newspaper with “CHAMPS!” stamped above his photo.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Eagles deny the Chiefs a Super Bowl three-peat with dominant defense in a 40-22 rout
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Yo Philly: Jalen Hurts and Nick Sirianni are world champions. A ferocious Philadelphia Eagles defense tormented and frustrated Patrick Mahomes while Hurts made all the plays the offense needed. So much for the Kansas City Chiefs’ quest for a Super Bowl three-peat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5
